second_close_attempt_note: This is the SECOND /close attempt for task-id 20260519-175339. The prior attempt concluded CLOSE: NO due to QA-test orphans (now cleared). The diff under audit is IDENTICAL to the prior /close — same baseline snapshot, same 245-line current file, same 104-117 (additive-only) added line range. This report content matches the prior version because the underlying audit input is unchanged.

diff_scope.scope_note: Per spec-20260503-091826 §5.4 rule 3, cleanliness audit is restricted to lines NEW in this diff. Pre-existing lines (1-103 and 218-245 of current file mapping to snapshot lines 1-103 and 104-130) are out-of-scope.

standards_evaluated.rationale: Rule scopes to .claude/commands/*.md and .claude/agents/*.md. The audited file is a bash script at /usr/local/bin/, not a command/agent markdown file.

All added hardcoded values (LOCK_FILE='/run/playwright-mcp.lock' L112; 30s flock timeout L122; 1s curl --max-time/--connect-timeout L152; python3 heredoc constants timeout=1/timeout=2/3.0/0.5 L164,187,201,204; exit 75 L118,124) are explicitly locked-design invariants per BA spec docs/dev/ticket-20260519-175339.md. The probe URL is argv-driven via $CDP_URL (resolved at L135-143), satisfying codex BA-iteration-2 review finding 1 which expressly required NO hardcoded http://127.0.0.1:8080 fallback. Parameterizing LOCK_FILE would defeat the cross-session contention semantics (all sessions must contend on the SAME file). Comments at L104-111 and L127-134 document the design rationale inline.

python3 heredoc at L156 is an intentional locked-design choice per task brief: this is a system-level wrapper at /usr/local/bin/ that runs before any project context is established; there is no project venv to source at this scope. Standard 3 targets project script Python invocations, not system wrappers in /usr/local/bin/. The heredoc payload uses only stdlib modules (json, sys, time, urllib.request, urllib.error) so no third-party dependency triggers a venv requirement.
Rule scopes to step-numbered .md files in .claude/. The audited file is a bash script.

New identifiers (LOCK_FILE, CDP_URL, prev, cdp_url, list_pages, is_stale_page, close_target, targets, ids, deadline, remaining, cur, cur_ids, tid, req, r, data, t) are all descriptive of purpose. No forbidden patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, *-v2, optimize-*, improved-*, etc.).
All added comments, error strings, and stderr messages (L104-217) are English. Grep for Unicode range U+4E00-U+9FFF on the file returned zero matches.
standards_evaluated.unconditional_scan_note: Standard 6's unconditional scan applies to .claude/commands/*.md and .claude/agents/*.md. The audited file is outside that scope so the exception does not widen the audit here.

standards_evaluated.rationale: No new scripts created. Modifications are confined to the single existing wrapper /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth.

Rule scopes to .claude/commands/*.md and .claude/agents/*.md verbosity. Even applied advisorily: comments in the diff (L104-111, L113-115, L120-121, L127-134, L145-149, L151-155, L215-217) are functional design rationale documenting R1-R6 constraints, FD_CLOEXEC inheritance semantics, the AC12 anti-cheat constraint forbidding kill/pkill/fuser/lsof, exit-code semantics, and the trap-vs-exec rationale. No marketing language, no story-style prose, no repetitive examples, no pseudocode blocks.

The variable input (CDP endpoint URL) is parameterized via argv parsing at L135-143, satisfying codex BA-iteration-2 finding 1. Locked-design constants (lock file path, timeouts, exit code) are spec-fixed invariants per docs/dev/ticket-20260519-175339.md — Standard 9's intent is to surface values that 'could reasonably differ between runs'; these values are spec-locked precisely because they MUST NOT differ across runs (shared lock contention, deterministic timeout, POSIX EX_TEMPFAIL signaling).

Added code references only system binaries available on PATH (curl, python3, flock — all standard Ubuntu utilities) and the pre-existing /usr/bin/playwright-mcp invocation (L218, out-of-diff). No .py / .sh script references introduced; no import statements that could dangle.
Grep for ^(<{7}|={7}|>{7}) against /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth returned zero matches.

advisory_observations.finding: Comment references 'line 126-127' but those lines shifted to 241-242 in the current file after the diff was applied.
advisory_observations.rationale_non_blocking: Standard 10 (dangling-references) targets script/import references that cause runtime failures, not comment-internal documentation pointers. This is a documentation-accuracy nit that does not affect cleanliness gating.

advisory_observations.finding: Python heredoc imports 'os' but never uses it (import json, os, sys, time, urllib.request, urllib.error).
advisory_observations.rationale_non_blocking: No /dev style standard in agents/style-inspector.md targets unused imports; this is a tidiness concern, not a quality-gate violation.

diff_aware_classification.scope_contract: Per agents/style-inspector.md --changed-files mode contract: findings tagged with file:line so close.md AC-2.6(b) can perform diff-overlap + pre-diff-baseline check. Pre-edit baseline supplied at /tmp/pre-cycle.playwright-mcp-stealth.snapshot was used to compute the diff and confirm all evaluated content falls within L104-217 of the current file. Zero new violations means there is nothing for close.md to classify NEW vs pre-existing — the empty violations[] list is unambiguous.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: PreToolUse:Skill hook blocked the Skill(codex) invocation with tool-policy.v1: {"role":"style-inspector","tool":"Skill","target":null,"deny_reason":"tool Skill not in allowed_tools"}. The style-inspector role's allowed_tools list does not include the Skill tool, so codex consultation could not be performed in-band.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — performed 5-axis adversarial pass: (1) false-positive scan of locked-design dismissals, (2) missed-violation scan of L104-217 including word-splitting, unused imports, stale line-number references, (3) N/A scoping verification for standards 1/4/8, (4) severity calibration check, (5) standard-applicability completeness. Result: draft verdict (zero violations) stands. Two advisory observations logged as observation_only (comment line-number staleness at L111; unused 'os' import at L157); neither is a /dev style-standard violation.
